persons.arguments_against: HENRY, R. is a single-initial bio and the surname is common (74 staff-list officials). But the position 'Superintendent of telegraphs' is a unique colony office (one holder), the colony (Tasmania) and the 1878 appointment continuing into the 1888-1894 listing all agree; a same-initial namesake holding the identical specialised telegraph post in the same colony is implausible.
persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person in the same colony and years holding the same office, or a printed death/birth date excluding these years.
